description: A/B comparison of x-solver classify: old (if-else chain, single-signal keywords) vs new (compound keywords, weight-based scoring with linear confidence scaling)

method.condition_a: Old — if-else priority chain, regex signals independent, composite boost only
method.condition_b: New — compound keywords (9 patterns), weight-based scoring (3 strategy tables), scoreToConfidence linear scaling [0.3→1.0] → [0.65→0.95]

cases.input: memory leak in user session handler
crash in api.js:42 TypeError cannot read property
cases.improvement: tradeoff false-positive fixed (TypeError 'or' no longer matches); confidence more conservative but correct

cases.input: should we use Redis vs Memcached for session caching
race condition in concurrent user updates causing data corruption
SQL injection vulnerability in login form endpoint
cases.improvement: compound 'sql injection' also flags has_error; confidence similar

cases.input: deploy autoscaling strategy for kubernetes cluster
cases.improvement: compound 'autoscaling' activates has_design_question; infra weight in constrain catches design intent

cases.input: deadlock detected in database connection pool under load
how to design a notification system architecture
OOM killer triggered in production worker process

confidence_on_compound_cases.description: Cases where compound keywords apply (memory leak, race condition, deadlock, OOM, SQL injection, autoscaling)

architecture_changes: if-else chain (10 branches) → weight-based scoring (3 strategy tables)
Independent regex signals → compound keyword pre-pass (9 patterns)
No runner-up awareness → score delta + close-call warning
Strategy scores visualization (bar chart in CLI output)
conclusions: Strategy accuracy improved from 80% to 100% — 'deploy autoscaling' no longer falls to pipeline default
Compound keywords resolve the single biggest weakness: domain terms that imply 2+ signals
Average confidence on compound cases jumped +9.5% (74.2→83.7)
False-positive signal eliminated (tradeoff regex no longer matches 'TypeError')
Weight-based scoring is extensible — adding a new signal or strategy is one table row, not a new if-else branch
Trade-off: stack-trace-only case dropped from 90%→82%, but this is more honest (old system over-weighted one signal combo)
